CRTMonitor.author: pend00
frag: Shader from Godot Shaders - the free shader library.
This shader is under CC0 license. Feel free to use, improve and
change this shader according to your needs and consider sharing
// - If you are using this shader to affect the node it is applied to set 'overlay' to false (unchecked in the instepctor).
// - If you are using this shader as an overlay, and want the shader to affect the nodes below in the Scene hierarchy,
//   set 'overlay' to true (checked in the inspector).
// On Mac there is potentially a bug causing this to not work properly. If that is the case and you want to use the shader as an overlay
// change all "overlay ? SCREEN_TEXTURE : TEXTURE" to only "SCREEN_TEXTURE" on lines 129-140, and "vec2 uv = overlay ? warp(SCREEN_UV) : warp(UV);"
//uniform vec2 resolution = vec2(640.0, 480.0); // Set the number of rows and columns the texture will be divided in. Scanlines and grille will make a square based on these values
float pixelate = 1.0; // Fill each square ("pixel") with a sampled color, creating a pixel look and a more accurate representation of how a CRT monitor would work.
float roll_speed = 8.0; // Positive values are down, negative are up
float roll_variation = clamp( 1.8, 0.1, 5.0); // This valie is not an exact science. You have to play around with the value to find a look you like. How this works is explained in the code below.
float distort_intensity = clamp( 0.05, 0.0, 0.2); // The distortion created by the rolling effect.
float noise_speed = 5.0; // There is a movement in the noise pattern that can be hard to see first. This sets the speed of that movement.
float aberration = clamp(0.03, -1.0, 1.0); // Chromatic aberration, a distortion on each color channel.
float brightness = 1.4; // When adding scanline gaps and grille the image can get very dark. Brightness tries to compensate for that.
float discolor = 1.0; // Add a discolor effect simulating a VHS
float warp_amount = clamp(1.0, 0.0, 5.0); // Warp the texture edges simulating the curved glass of a CRT monitor or old TV.
float vignette_intensity = 0.4; // Size of the vignette, how far towards the middle it should go.
// Used by the noise functin to generate a pseudo random value between 0.0 and 1.0
// Generate a Perlin noise used by the distortion effects
return mix( mix( dot( random(uv_index + vec2(0.0,0.0) ), uv_fract - vec2(0.0,0.0) ),
dot( random(uv_index + vec2(1.0,0.0) ), uv_fract - vec2(1.0,0.0) ), blur.x),
mix( dot( random(uv_index + vec2(0.0,1.0) ), uv_fract - vec2(0.0,1.0) ),
dot( random(uv_index + vec2(1.0,1.0) ), uv_fract - vec2(1.0,1.0) ), blur.x), blur.y) * 0.5 + 0.5;
// Takes in the UV and warps the edges, creating the spherized effect
// Adds a black border to hide stretched pixel created by the warp effect
// Adds a vignette shadow to the edges of the image
void mainImage( out vec4 fragColor, in vec2 texCoord ) {
//vec2 uv = overlay ? warp(SCREEN_UV) : warp(UV); // Warp the uv. uv will be used in most cases instead of UV to keep the warping
// Pixelate the texture based on the given resolution.
// Create the rolling effect. We need roll_line a bit later to make the noise effect.
// That is why this runs if roll is true OR noise_opacity is over 0.
// Create the areas/lines where the texture will be distorted.
// Create more lines of a different size and apply to the first set of lines. This creates a bit of variation.
roll_line *= roll_line * smoothstep(0.3, 0.9, sin(uv.y * roll_size * roll_variation - (time * roll_speed * roll_variation) ) );
// Distort the UV where where the lines are
// If roll is true distort the texture with roll_uv. The texture is split up into RGB to
// make some chromatic aberration. We apply the aberration to the red and green channels accorging to the aberration parameter
// and intensify it a bit in the roll distortion.
// If roll is false only apply the aberration without any distorion. The aberration values are very small so the .1 is only
// to make the slider in the Inspector less sensitive.
// CRT monitors don't have pixels but groups of red, green and blue dots or lines, called grille. We isolate the texture's color channels
// and divide it up in 3 offsetted lines to show the red, green and blue colors next to each other, with a small black gap between.
// Apply the grille to the texture's color channels and apply Brightness. Since the grille and the scanlines (below) make the image very dark you
// can compensate by increasing the brightness.
// Scanlines are the horizontal lines that make up the image on a CRT monitor.
// Here we are actual setting the black gap between each line, which I guess is not the right definition of the word, but you get the idea
// Same technique as above, create lines with sine and applying it to the texture. Smoothstep to allow setting the line size.
// Generate a noise pattern that is very stretched horizontally, and animate it with noise_speed
float noise = smoothstep(0.4, 0.5, noise(uv * vec2(2.0, 200.0) + vec2(10.0, (iTime * (noise_speed))) ) );
// We use roll_line (set above) to define how big the noise should be vertically (multiplying cuts off all black parts).
// We also add in some basic noise with random() to break up the noise pattern above. The noise is sized according to
// the resolution value set in the inspector. If you don't like this look you can
// change "ceil(uv * resolution) / resolution" to only "uv" to make it less pixelated. Or multiply resolution with som value
// greater than 1.0 to make them smaller.
roll_line *= noise * scanlines * clamp(random((ceil(uv * resolution) / resolution) + vec2(iTime * 0.8, 0.0)).x + 0.8, 0.0, 1.0);
// Add it to the texture based on noise_opacity
text.rgb = clamp(mix(text.rgb, text.rgb + roll_line, noise_opacity), vec3(0.0), vec3(1.0));
// Apply static noise by generating it over the whole screen in the same way as above
text.rgb += clamp(random((ceil(uv * resolution) / resolution) + fract(iTime)).x, 0.0, 1.0) * static_noise_intensity;
// Apply a black border to hide imperfections caused by the warping.
// Hides the black border and make that area transparent. Good if you want to add the the texture on top an image of a TV or monitor.
// Apply discoloration to get a VHS look (lower saturation and higher contrast)
// You can play with the values below or expose them in the Inspector.

vec3 mod289(vec3 x) { return x - floor(x * (1.0 / 289.0)) * 289.0; }
vec4 mod289(vec4 x) { return x - floor(x * (1.0 / 289.0)) * 289.0; }
vec3 x0 = v - i + dot(i, C.xxx);
vec4 p = permute( permute( permute( i.z + vec4(0.0, i1.z, i2.z, 1.0)) + i.y + vec4(0.0, i1.y, i2.y, 1.0 )) + i.x + vec4(0.0, i1.x, i2.x, 1.0 ));
// Gradients: 7x7 points over a square, mapped onto an octahedron.
// The ring size 17*17 = 289 is close to a multiple of 49 (49*6 = 294)
vec4 j = p - 49.0 * floor(p * ns.z * ns.z);  //  mod(p,7*7)
vec4 norm = taylorInvSqrt(vec4(dot(p0,p0), dot(p1,p1), dot(p2, p2), dot(p3,p3)));
vec4 m = max(0.6 - vec4(dot(x0,x0), dot(x1,x1), dot(x2,x2), dot(x3,x3)), 0.0);
return 42.0 * dot( m*m, vec4( dot(p0,x0), dot(p1,x1), dot(p2,x2), dot(p3,x3)));

float focus = S(.9,1.,ti); // Step into focus while Dist between .8 and 1.
float size = mix(.05,.03,focus);             // reduce size when in focus
// defn of pt "p" moved inside Bokeh parameters ,
float ref = 0.;  // define faint road reflection. Note .9 blur
m += ref * focus;                            // Add reflection when in focus
// return vec3(.9,.9,1.) * m;              // define color blue-white
return bulb * m;                        // CDA define bulb color mostly blue-white
t /=4.;                  // Overtaken traffic ¼ as frequent as streetlights
float w1 = .25;          // Define width of a car
float w2 = w1 * 1.2;     // Define width of a car headlight
float s = 1./6.;         // Car frequency = 6 per sec
vec3 bulb;               // CDA defined outside loop - mutant car bulb
float n = N(i);            // for each car get rnd between 0 and 1
if (n>.5) continue;        // skip to next car for 50% of virtual cars
bulb = vec3(1.,.15,.025);    // CDA set normal bulb color RED was (1.,.1,.1)
/* Trick : reducing blue from .1 to .03 gives orange blinker eg. Original (1.,.1,.03) -
Orange = -25% Green -75% Blue. From Red's Brightness .1 -> .075g .025b,
/* doesn't work as NR(i) etc fixed, only evaluated at compile time ?
if (NR(i)<.02) {                    // CDA Probability of mutant bulb
bulb = vec3(NR(i),NG(i),NB(i));  // CDA set random bulb color
float lane = step(.25,n);  // returns 0 or 1 (as n range is now 0 to .5)
float ti = fract(t+i);     // fract resets posn,
float fade = pow(ti,5.);   // ti^5 causes dist. fade as light recedes
//   float fade = ti*ti*ti*ti*ti;  // Not using pow(ti,5) reintroduces BUG
float focus = S(.9,1.,ti); // Step into focus while Dist between .9 and 1.
float size = mix(.05,.03,focus);      // reduce size when in focus
float laneshift = S(1.,.96,ti);       // 1 if far away 0 if close
float x = 1.5-(lane*laneshift);       // car position (was -1. for headlights)
float tmp3=7.*(lane-.00000001);       // BUG Fix requires var & *..01 Only lane 2
float blink = step(0.,sin(t*1000.))*tmp3*step(.796,ti);  // Blinker, off in dist @ .796 was .96
// defn of pt "p" is now vec3() moved inside Bokeh parameters ,
float ref = 0.;  // define faint road reflection. Note .9 blur
ref += Bokeh(r,vec3(x-w2,-.15,z),size*3.01,.9,1.,1.,.8)*fade; // Def refl 1 BUG Fixed with 3.01
// Def refl2 & blink - Bug *1.0000001 but * 1.00000001 OK
m += ref * focus;                            // Add reflection when in focus
// return vec3(1.,.1,.1) * m;              // define color red
return bulb * m;                        // CDA define bulb color mostly Red
//  vec2 st=uv*10.;     // #1 Rasta flag as ¼'s G,Y,R,Black clockwise from TopLeft
//  vec2 st=fract(uv*10.);     // #2 makes grid boxes 10 high
vec2 a = vec2(3.,1.);   // #3 makes boxes 3 * taller
st.y +=t*.22;           // Makes entire fame move down, .22 tweak cancels up motion
uv.y += n;              //     offsets drops vertically from centre of box
//  uv.x +=sin(n*.8*t/19.); // pow(n*.6,2.);   // CDA offsets drops horizontally from centre of box
st = fract(st)-.5;   // #3 within each box uv coords from (0,0) to (1,1),
float y =-sin(t+sin(t+sin(t)*.5))*.43; // #3 Adds control of vertical posn. of drop
// Above modification approximates a saw tooth wave, up quick down slow
vec2 p1 = vec2(0.,y);        // #3 .43 above is tweak to prevent drop overlapping its box
float d = length(o1);   // #3 distance to droplet ,/a fixes ellipses
float m1 = S(.07,.0,d);     // #3 mask for droplet (yellow on black,only RG cols used!)
vec2 o2 = (fract(uv*a.x*vec2(1.,2.))-.5)/vec2(1.,2.); // #4 Trail of smaller drops,
float m2 = S(.3*(.5-st.y),.0,d)*S(-.1,.1,st.y-p1.y); // #4 mask for smaller droplets
